seo.title: Privacy-First Architecture tools directory
seo.description: A developer-first directory of Privacy-First Architecture tools and references.

content.intro: A curated directory of privacy-respecting tools and infrastructure providers designed for developers who prioritize data sovereignty, user trust, and GDPR compliance.

entries.name: Plausible Analytics
entries.description: Lightweight and open-source website analytics that doesn't use cookies and is fully compliant with GDPR, CCPA, and PECR.
cons: No free tier for hosted version
Limited depth for complex event tracking

entries.name: Umami
entries.description: A simple, fast, privacy-focused alternative to Google Analytics that can be self-hosted with a single database.
cons: Requires manual database maintenance
Fewer third-party integrations than Plausible

entries.name: Hetzner Cloud
entries.description: German-based cloud hosting provider offering high-performance VPS and dedicated servers within EU data centers.
pros: Strict adherence to German privacy laws
cons: Fewer managed services than AWS/GCP
Identity verification process can be strict

entries.name: Listmonk
entries.description: Self-hosted newsletter and mailing list manager with a focus on performance and data privacy.
pros: Supports custom S3 providers for media
cons: Requires managing your own SMTP relay
No built-in drag-and-drop template editor

entries.name: LocalAI
entries.description: A drop-in replacement REST API that is compatible with OpenAI API specifications for local AI inference.
pros: Keep sensitive data on your own hardware
No API keys or internet connection required
Supports LLMs, image generation, and audio
cons: Requires significant GPU or CPU resources
Complex initial model configuration

entries.name: PostHog (Self-Hosted)
entries.description: Product OS that includes analytics, heatmaps, and feature flags, deployable on your own infrastructure.
pros: Unified tool for product and engineering
cons: High infrastructure overhead for large scale
Complex deployment via Kubernetes

entries.name: Scaleway
entries.description: European cloud provider offering a wide range of services including S3-compatible storage and managed Kubernetes.
cons: Documentation can be sparse for niche features
Support response times vary by tier

entries.name: Buttondown
entries.description: A minimalist, privacy-first newsletter tool designed for developers, with strong support for Markdown.
cons: Limited visual design customization
Paid tier required for multiple newsletters

entries.name: HashiCorp Vault
entries.description: Manage secrets and protect sensitive data by providing a central place to store and control access to tokens and passwords.
cons: Steep learning curve for configuration
Operational overhead for high availability

entries.name: Ollama
entries.description: Get up and running with large language models locally, providing a simple CLI and API for private AI integration.
pros: Extremely easy setup on macOS and Linux
Zero data leakage to third-party providers
cons: Limited to local machine or private network
No built-in user authentication
